authoritative_sources.title: 31 CFR Part 1020, Rules for Banks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal AML rule set for banks covering customer identification, suspicious transaction reporting, due diligence, and recordkeeping requirements that directly constrain financial-crimes operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Alert intake, customer and transaction truth validation, and suspicious-activity escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Electronic Fund Transfers FAQs
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CFPB guidance clarifying error-resolution and unauthorized-transfer obligations that materially affect consumer-impact handling when a financial-crimes case involves payment disputes or holds.
used_for: customer communication and reimbursement boundary framing

authoritative_sources.title: FFIEC BSA/AML Assessing Compliance with BSA Regulatory Requirements - Customer Identification Program
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official interagency examination procedures for CIP controls, including what institutions must do when identity evidence is missing or contradictory.
used_for: blocked handling when reasonable belief of identity is not met
adjacent-lane routing to KYC or AML owners

authoritative_sources.title: FFIEC BSA/AML Assessing Compliance with BSA Regulatory Requirements - Suspicious Activity Reporting
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official interagency examination guidance defining monitoring, alert management, escalation, documentation, and SAR-related workflow expectations relevant to financial-crimes operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Alert triage, suspicious-activity documentation, and escalation
used_for: refusal of unsupported clearance or closure

authoritative_sources.title: Suspicious Activity Report Supporting Documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FinCEN guidance defining what SAR-supporting documentation includes, how it is retained, and how disclosure is limited.

authoritative_sources.title: FFIEC BSA/AML Appendices - Appendix P – BSA Record Retention Requirements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FFIEC appendix summarizing retention periods for BSA-relevant customer, transaction, funds-transfer, SAR, CTR, and CIP records relied on by financial-crimes operations.
used_for: closeout refusal when evidence obligations are not met

authoritative_sources.title: Sanctions List Service
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Office of Foreign Assets Control
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OFAC's official list service is the authoritative current source for sanctions-screening data used when deciding whether a financial workflow must stop for sanctions escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Sanctions screening and blocked workflow review
used_for: safe refusal of unsupported release requests

authoritative_sources.title: A Framework for OFAC Compliance Commitments
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Office of Foreign Assets Control
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official OFAC control framework describing risk assessment, internal controls, testing, and escalation components that shape sanctions-sensitive operational workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Sanctions control design and escalation quality
used_for: control expectations for sanctions workflow handling

authoritative_sources.title: ACH Network Risk and Enforcement Topics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Public Nacha rules guidance is authoritative for ACH unauthorized return codes, return-rate concepts, and risk-enforcement terminology used in ACH exceptions that intersect with financial-crimes case handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ACH return, denial, and exception review

authoritative_sources.title: Internal Routine and Controls, Section 4.2 of the Risk Management Manual of Examination Policies
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDIC examination policy is authoritative for segregation of duties, documentation, approval, and operational-control expectations in bank workflows where suspicious or exception items affect posting and reconciliation.
used_for: control checkpoints around posting and adjustment changes

authoritative_sources.title: Anti-Money Laundering Program and Suspicious Activity Reporting Requirements For Insurance Companies Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FinCEN guidance describing AML and suspicious-activity obligations for covered insurance products, which is relevant when tenant workflows include cash-value insurance or annuities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Entity-type determination and insurance-specific applicability review
used_for: non-blocking ambiguity handling for mixed financial-services tenants

workflow_stages.name: Lane classification and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Identify whether the case is bank-payment, card, wire, ACH, lending, or covered-insurance workflow work and verify the minimum entity, product, jurisdiction, owner, and source-of-truth prerequisites before proceeding.

workflow_stages.name: Transaction, account, and case truth-set assembly
workflow_stages.description: Assemble authoritative transaction, account, payment, alert, KYC/CIP, sanctions, approval, and communication records into a traceable evidence package that explains the current state.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Control-aware workflow impact assessment
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether financial-crimes controls support a hold, block, denial, release package, posting recommendation, or refusal while respecting customer-impact and entity-specific obligations.

workflow_stages.name: Reconciliation, denial, and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Compare ledger, payment-hub, return, screening, and case outcomes to produce matched items, blocked items, denied items, unresolved variances, and escalation notes instead of unsupported corrections.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Escalation and retention-safe closeout
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when suspicious activity, sanctions, consumer-impact, legal interpretation, or mixed-source conflicts change the outcome, and preserve the required evidence and retention posture.

artifacts: Account, policy, transaction, application, payment, claim, wire, card, or ACH records
Transaction-monitoring alerts, sanctions-screening outputs, KYC, CIP, CDD, beneficial-owner, and case-history artifacts
Unauthorized-transfer, dispute, hold, restriction, denial, and approval-history records
Customer communication logs, notices, disclosures, servicing notes, and escalation packets
Reconciliation workpapers, general-ledger extracts, payment-hub results, return records, and variance logs
Tenant policy extracts covering entity type, product applicability, approval thresholds, escalation ownership, and source precedence
systems_of_record: Core banking, card, wire, ACH, or payment processing platform
Policy administration or covered-insurance transaction platform when applicable
Transaction monitoring, sanctions screening, fraud monitoring, and AML case systems
Case management, CRM, and document management systems
General ledger, reconciliation tooling, and regulatory reporting systems

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked based on financial-crimes operational ownership.
Validate whether retrieved records support a hold, blocked status, exception package, posting recommendation, reconciliation note, or escalation outcome.
Prepare traceable workflow summaries, reconciliation notes, and escalation packets with explicit uncertainty and next-owner routing.
escalate: Entity type, product type, jurisdiction, or control owner is missing and would change the result
Account, payment, sanctions, or case records disagree
Fraud, AML, sanctions, unauthorized-transfer, or consumer-harm signals are present
Release, reimbursement, adjustment, denial, or closure requires legal, underwriting, approval, or specialized compliance ownership
refuse: Requests to create or alter transaction, account, case, or approval facts without source evidence
Requests for SAR judgment, sanctions clearance, legal interpretation, reimbursement approval, account closure approval, or regulated advice
Requests to treat financial crimes as a generic finance label and skip entity, product, or source-of-truth context

domain_failure_modes: Posting, releasing, or adjusting a financial outcome that is not supported by authoritative transaction, case, screening, or approval records
Treating alerts, sanctions matches, or identity discrepancies as resolved without the required specialist owner

Using generic finance terminology that hides financial-crimes artifacts such as CIP evidence, SAR-supporting documentation, sanctions screening results, unauthorized return codes, or blocked-item history
Proceeding when public guidance, tenant policy, or systems-of-record conflict on whether a case may be released, adjusted, or closed
Failing to preserve required evidence, retention, or communication artifacts for suspicious-activity and customer-impact cases
Flattening banking and covered-insurance workflows into one rule path when entity-specific obligations materially differ

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Source records do not support the requested billing or adjustment
Reconciliation mismatch or denial requires exception handling
Stakeholder asks for an unsupported financial override

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Entity-type applicability across banking and insurance workflows
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Bank AML, sanctions, and consumer-payment rules do not apply uniformly to every insurer or financial-services affiliate. The pack requires entity type and product context before it will choose a confident handling path.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific sanctions-screening thresholds and match-resolution procedures
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public OFAC sources define the authoritative list and control framework but not each institution's scoring, fuzzy-match, or escalation workflow. The pack treats these as tenant-supplied controls and escalates if they are missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Customer-impact timing and reimbursement deltas by product
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Unauthorized-transfer, card, ACH, wire, and covered-insurance workflows can carry different customer-impact and remediation rules. The pack records this as a tenant and product retrieval dependency instead of guessing.

notes: The source set intentionally mixes primary regulators, official examination manuals, public payment workflow rules, and public control frameworks so workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling are all covered.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because the unresolved items are explicitly converted into tenant-required retrieval facts and escalation triggers.
